Your task is to review the following Terraform code and identify any security misconfigurations.

You must find AT LEAST ONE valid Checkov vulnerability ID (e.g. CKV_AWS_20) that applies to the code. Finding more will increase your score.
You must also provide syntactically valid Terraform HCL code that remediates the issue.

You MUST output your findings strictly in the following JSON format. Do not add any text before or after the JSON block:

  "vulnerabilities": ["CKV_AWS_123", "..."],
  "remediation_code": "resource \"aws_s3_bucket\" {\n  # valid HCL fix\n}"
}

Terraform Code:
# Create a new load balancer
resource "aws_elb" "weblb" {
  name = "weblb-terraform-elb"

  listener {
    instance_port     = 8000
    instance_protocol = "http"
    lb_port           = 80
    lb_protocol       = "http"
  }

  health_check {
    healthy_threshold   = 2
    unhealthy_threshold = 2
    timeout             = 3
    target              = "HTTP:8000/"
    interval            = 30
  }

  subnets                     = [aws_subnet.web_subnet.id]
  security_groups             = [aws_security_group.web-node.id]
  instances                   = [aws_instance.web_host.id]
  cross_zone_load_balancing   = true
  idle_timeout                = 400
  connection_draining         = true
  connection_draining_timeout = 400

  tags = merge({
    Name = "foobar-terraform-elb"
    }, {
    git_commit           = "d68d2897add9bc2203a5ed0632a5cdd8ff8cefb0"
    git_file             = "terraform/aws/elb.tf"
    git_last_modified_at = "2020-06-16 14:46:24"
    git_last_modified_by = "nimrodkor@gmail.com"
    git_modifiers        = "nimrodkor"
    git_org              = "bridgecrewio"
    git_repo             = "terragoat"

}
